meaning: vt.鍒跺畾(娉曞緥),寤虹珛(鏀垮簻),缁勬垚,浠诲懡
examples: China's ethnic minorities constitute less than 7 percent of its total population.
Testing patients without their consent would constitute a professional and legal offence.
Both of these lobes together constitute a pi bond.
The records of the Company are therefore precious, and constitute part of the memory of the world.
Hedge funds constitute private pools of capital, with investors meeting certain net worth or sophistication requirements.
